title: asperula aristata subsp. umbellulata
intro: Asperula aristata subsp. umbellulata, commonly known as Woodruff, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and sweet, earthy fragrance. Traditionally, Woodruff has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, as well as its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Woodruff include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote better sleep. Woodruff can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
